questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Match the following
$\begin{array}{|c|l|c|l|} \hline &\text{ Column I} & & \text{Column II} \\ \hline \text{(A)} & \text{The function } f(x)=-x^4 e^{-2} \text{ is strictly decreasing in} & (p) & (1 / 3, \infty) \\ \hline \text{(B)} & \begin{array}{l} \text{The centre of circle passing through the points } (0,0), \\ (1,0) \text{ and touching the circle } x^2+y^2=9 \text{ is} \end{array} & (q) & (0,4) \\ \hline \text{(C)} & \begin{array}{l} \text{The real values of a for which the point } (-2 a, a+1) \\ \text{ lies in the smaller region bounded by the circle } \\ x^2+y^2=4 \text{ and the parabola } y^2=4 x \text{ belong to} \end{array} & (r) & \left(\frac{1}{2} \cdot \sqrt{2}\right) \\ \hline \text{(D)} & \text{If } \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{6 x}{1+9 x^2}\right)=-\frac{\pi}{2}+2 \tan ^{-1} 3 x, \text{ then } x \in & (s) & (-\infty, 0) \\ \hline\end{array}$
questions.solution:
(A) $f^{\prime}(x) \lt 0 \Rightarrow+x^1 c^{-x}-4 x^3 c^{-x} \lt 0$
$x^3 e^{-x}(x-4) \lt 0$
(B) $\mathrm{c}=0, \mathrm{~g}=-\frac{1}{2}$ as $\mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{y}^2+2 \mathrm{gx}+2 \mathrm{fy}+\mathrm{c}=0$ passes through $(0,0),(1,0)$ and given touches $x^2+y^2=9$
(C) If the point $(-2 \mathrm{a}, \mathrm{a}+1)$ lies in shaded region
$\begin{aligned}
& 4 a^2+(a+1)^2-4 \lt 0 \text { and }(a+1)^2-4(-2 a) \lt 0 \\
& \Rightarrow 5 a^2+2 a-3 \lt 0 \text { and } a^2+10 a+1 \lt 0 \Rightarrow a \in(-1,-5+2 \sqrt{6})
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}
&\text {(D) }  \frac{\pi}{2}-\sin ^{-1} \frac{6 x}{1+9 x^2}=-\frac{\pi}{2}+2 \tan ^{-1} 3 x \Rightarrow \sin ^{-1} \frac{6 x}{1+9 x^2}=\pi-2 \tan ^1 3 x \\
\Rightarrow & \sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{2(3 x)}{1+(3 x)^2}\right]=\pi-2 \tan ^{-1} 3 x \Rightarrow 3 x \gt 1
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\left|f^{\prime \prime}(x)\right| \leq 1 \forall x \in R$ and $f(0)=0=f^{\prime}(0)$ then which of the following can be TRUE?
questions.solution:
According to LMVT
$\begin{aligned}
& \left|\frac{f^{\prime}(x)-f^{\prime}(0)}{x}\right|=\left|f^{\prime \prime}\left(C_1\right)\right| \leq 1 \\
& \Rightarrow\left|f^{\prime}(x)\right| \leq|x| \\
& \text { again by LMVT } \quad\left|\frac{f(x)-f(0)}{x}\right|=\left|f^{\prime}\left(C_2\right)\right| \leq|x| \\
& \Rightarrow f(x) \leq x^2
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the slope of tangent to curve $y=e^x \cos x$ possess local maxima at $x=a$, then 'a' equals -
questions.solution:
$\frac{d y}{d x}=e^x(\cos x-\sin x)=m \quad$ (let)
Now $\frac{d m}{d x}=-2 e^x \sin x$
For m to be max., $\frac{\mathrm{dm}}{\mathrm{dx}}=0 \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{n} \pi, \mathrm{n} \in \mathrm{I}$
$\frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{~m}}{\mathrm{dx}^2}=-2 e^{\mathrm{x}}(\sin \mathrm{x}+\cos \mathrm{x})$
At $\mathrm{x}=0, \frac{\mathrm{~d}^2 \mathrm{~m}}{\mathrm{dx}^2} \lt 0$
$\therefore \quad \mathrm{x}=0$ is a point of maxima
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The curve, with the property that the projection of the ordinate on the normal is constant and has a length equal to 'a', is
questions.solution:
$\text { Ordinate }=\mathrm{PM} \text {. Let } \mathrm{P} \equiv(\mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y})$ Projection of ordinate on normal = PN $\therefore \quad \mathrm{PN}=\mathrm{PM} \cos \theta=\mathrm{a}$ (given)
$\therefore \frac{y}{\sqrt{1+\tan ^2 \theta}}=a \Rightarrow y=a \sqrt{1+\left(y_1\right)^2}$ $\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sqrt{y^2-a^2}}{a} \Rightarrow \int \frac{a d y}{\sqrt{y^2-a^2}}=\int d x \Rightarrow a \ln \left|y+\sqrt{y^2-a^2}\right|=x+c$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $f(x)$ is defined for $x\gt-1$ and has a continuous derivate. If $f$ satisfies $f(0)=1, f^{\prime}(0)=0$; $(1+f(x)) f^{\prime \prime}(x)=1+x$. If $x$ is positive then $f^{\prime}(x)$ is
questions.solution: $f^{\prime \prime}(x)=\frac{1+x}{1+f(x)}$. Also $f^{\prime \prime}(x)\gt0 \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(x)$ is increasing $\Rightarrow f^{\prime}(x)\gtf^{\prime}(0)=0 \forall x\gt0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(x)$ is differentiable function with $f^{\prime}(0)=1$ and $\cos 2 x \sin 2 y(f(3 x+3 y)+f(3 x-3 y))=\sin 2 x \cos 2 y(f(3 x+3 y)-f(3 x-3 y))$ for all $x, b \in R$, then the equation of tangent to $y=f(x)$ at $x=\frac{\pi}{2}$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{f(3 x+3 y)}{\sin (2 x+3 y)}=\frac{f(3 x-3 y)}{\sin (2 x-2 y)} \forall x, y \in R \\
& \Rightarrow f(x)=k \sin \left(\frac{2 x}{3}\right) \\
& \therefore f^{\prime}(0)=\frac{2}{3} k=1 \Rightarrow k=\frac{3}{2} \\
& \therefore f(x)=\frac{3}{2} \sin \left(\frac{2 x}{3}\right) \Rightarrow f^{\prime}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=\frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{4}
\end{aligned}$
Equation of tangent is $y-\frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{4}=\frac{1}{2}\left(x-\frac{\pi}{2}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Match the following
$\begin{array}{|c|l|c|c|}\hline & \text{Column I} & \text{Column II} \\ \hline \text{(A)} & \begin{array}{l} \text{Number of points where the function } \\ f(x)=\max \{|\tan \pi x|, \cos \pi x\} \\ \text{ is non-differentiable in the interval } (-1,1) \end{array} & (p) & 6 \\ \hline \text{(B)} & \begin{array}{l} \text{The sum of those factors of 71} \\ \text{Which are of the form } 3 \mathrm{n}+1 \text{ and odd } (\mathrm{n} \in \mathrm{W}) \end{array} & (q) & 4 \\ \hline \text{(C)} & \begin{array}{l} \text{If } \mathrm{ax}^2+\mathrm{bx}+\mathrm{c}=0 \text{ and } 2 \mathrm{x}^2+3 \mathrm{x}+4=0 \\ \text{ have a common root where } a, b, c \in N, \\ \text{ then the least value of a+b+c is} \end{array} & (r) & 8 \\ \hline \text{(D)} & \begin{array}{l} \text{If } \int \tan ^7 \mathrm{xdx}=\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})+\log |\cos \mathrm{x}|, \\ \text{ then the degree of polynomial} f(x) \text{ in } \tan x \text{ is} \end{array} & (s) & 0 \\ \hline & & (t) & 9 \\ \hline\end{array}$
questions.solution: (A) Hence at 4 points function is not differentiable (B) $\because 7!=2^4 \cdot 3^2, 5,7$ and factor should be of $(3 n+1)$ form and odd. only. $\therefore$ their sum $=8$. $\left\{\because\right.$ in $2^\alpha 3^\beta 5^\gamma 7^\delta, \alpha=0$, $\beta=0, \gamma=0, \delta=1\}$ (C) $\because \ln 2 \mathrm{x}^2+3 \mathrm{x}+4=0$, roots are pair wise imaginary least of $a+b+c=2+3+4=9$ (D) $f(x)=\int \tan ^7 x d x-\log |\cos x|=\int \tan ^5 x\left(\sec ^2 x-1\right) d x-\log |\cos x|=\frac{\tan ^6 x}{6}-\int \tan ^5 x d x \ldots \ldots$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
